source_jobs.title: Enterprise Engagement Lead
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.




ROLE OVERVIEW

At Binti, the Project Management team leads the end-to-end delivery of our software with public sector partners, ensuring agencies successfully adopt Binti’s tools while transforming how they support children and families. We work closely with child welfare leaders, frontline staff, and cross-functional internal teams to translate complex, often ambiguous goals into well-executed solutions that drive positive outcomes for children and families.

As the Enterprise Engagement Lead, you’ll take ownership of our most strategic and high-impact enterprise launches, bringing technical fluency, critical problem-solving, passion for the mission, and executive presence to every phase of implementation. You’ll also collaborate closely with cross-functional leaders, including members of Binti’s executive team, to identify and lead high-priority initiatives that strengthen our implementation practices across the board. You’ll join a thoughtful, mission-driven team that values trust, autonomy, and continuous improvement as we scale Binti’s impact nationwide. This is a newly created, unique opportunity to shape how Binti scales its implementation model, bringing thought leadership, structure, and innovation to one of the most critical phases of our work.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own the full delivery of large-scale government implementations, including strategy, planning, stakeholder management, technical and programmatic execution, risk mitigation, and post-launch support

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for senior agency executives, aligning goals, setting expectations, and proactively resolving risks or misalignment

 - Engage effectively across all levels of agency partners, from frontline users to middle managers to agency leadership, with an adapted communication style and approach that builds trust, establishes clarity, and drives alignment.

 - Translate product capabilities and technical architecture (e.g. integrations, data pipelines, modular configurations, permission structures) into a fully implemented and configured solution that meets agency needs and aligns with Binti’s best practices.

 - Consult and help implement change management strategies, helping agency partners evolve outdated workflows and adopt new processes that align with Binti’s product and broader goals

 - Drive accountability across internal and external teams, ensuring clear communication, clarity of roles, visibility into blockers, and steady forward momentum across all phases of implementation

 - Contribute to the development of scalable systems and frameworks to elevate the PMO’s enterprise implementation model, with a focus on delivering high-quality, repeatable, and consultative professional services at scale.

 - Provide strategic implementation expertise during the pre

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.



OVERVIEW OF ROLE

We're looking for product oriented engineers who are skilled at leveraging AI both in their coding and in the products they build, and who are passionate about using technology to drive positive impact. In this role, you'll have the opportunity to take significant ownership of technical projects that will drive the success of the overall business. Strong candidates bring deep product and technical acumen, combined with the ability to move fast.



LOCATION

Binti is headquartered in San Francisco, California. Much of our team is local to the San Francisco Bay Area and works via a hybrid setup – in-person from the office on Monday & Thursday and remote otherwise. Satellite offices, like Seattle, Washington, set their own in-office days and hybrid policies, but in general employees are encouraged to join the leadership team on the days when they will be in the office for collaboration and team cohesion.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Lead architecture discussions during feature development to ensure that projects are built in a sound, reliable, performant way

 - Improve user experiences that software engineers and engineering teams depend on during their day-to-day workflow

 - Full Stack coding, 60% back end and 40% front end

 - Mentor other engineers on best practices for software engineering

 - Optimize the performance of components for both latency and throughput

 - Monitor all of the above in a scalable way

 - Guide incident response and vulnerability remediation efforts

 - Collaborate with Product Management to scope and execute product features while maintaining Binti’s values.

 - Focus on the longevity of the codebase - notice and advocate for patterns that allow code to be flexible, scalable, and approachable to other engineers

 - Help our team move quickly and learn while maintaining a welcoming and supportive culture

 - Push our product so it continues to be a beautiful, yet intuitive and accessible interface for users from all walks of life

 - Champion security and privacy, including looking for ways to make it easier to safeguard HIPAA compliance for sensitive data and incorporate security best practices



SAMPLE PROJECTS

 - Spearhead development of new product areas like child intake and community crisis response

 - Evolve configurable platform features like scheduling or application requirements into a reusable, performant layer that works for different workflows across the country

 - Architect engineering design for storing of configurable relationships between humans, beyond biological relationships

 - Transfer applications between agencies, accounting for variable workflow setups and agency-configured permissions



WHO YOU ARE

 - Experience leading and coordinating large infrastructural and architectural projects and rollouts

source_jobs.title: Integrations Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.



OVERVIEW OF ROLE

We’re seeking technically sharp, customer-focused engineers who are eager to collaborate closely with our partners to design and deliver integrations that maximize the full value of the Binti platform. As an Integrations Software Engineer and part of the Integrations Engineering team, you’ll take ownership of high-impact, externally-facing technical projects — from discovery and architecture sessions to launch. Strong candidates combine deep engineering experience with excellent communication skills, a service-oriented mindset, and the ability to thrive in dynamic and ambiguous environments.



LOCATION

Binti is headquartered in San Francisco, California. Much of our team is local to the San Francisco Bay Area and works via a hybrid setup – in-person from the office on Monday & Thursday and remote otherwise. Satellite offices, like Seattle, Washington, set their own in-office days and hybrid policies, but in general employees are encouraged to join the leadership team on the days when they will be in the office for collaboration and team cohesion.

A part of this position involves supporting our customers on their integration journey, and therefore, this job includes occasional travel to customer locations (approximately once a month). Opportunities to go on-site with our customers will include design and discovery sessions, co-development work, and technical onboarding.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Lead architecture discussions during integration development to ensure that they are built in a sound, reliable, and performant way

 - Optimize and improve the tools that our Binti’s internal delivery teams and our customers use to do their day-to-day work

 - Full Stack coding, 80% back end and 20% front end

 - Mentor other engineers on best practices for software engineering

 - Optimize the performance of integration pipelines, APIs, and components for both latency and throughput

 - Participate in discovery and architecture sessions with customers to ensure that integrations are designed to support our customers needs, best leverage the Binti platform, and be performant, secure, and scalable.

 - Build integration pipelines and APIs that are stable, performant, and scalable, and monitor them to ensure that they are operating properly

 - Work directly with our customers’ development teams, in person or virtually, to provide them live support and consulting as they integrate with the Binti platform and APIs.

 - Provide integration support to our customers, answering questions, offering consultative advice, and assisting them in debugging their integrations.

 - Synthesize and deliver product feedback to enable us to use it to improve Binti’s modules and integration capabilities.

 - Collaborate with Product Management to scope and execute product feature

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager/Senior Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.


POSITION SUMMARY

As an Engineering Manager at Binti, you will lead a talented and dedicated engineering team, fostering a culture of innovation, collaboration, and excellence. You will help set ambitious but achievable goals, design team-wide norms and processes to streamline operations, and coach your team to top performance. Ideally, you will bring a wealth of experience in SaaS product development, technical leadership, and a passion for leveraging technology to positively impact society.




CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Team Leadership: Lead and mentor a team of skilled engineers, fostering a culture of continuous learning, collaboration, accountability, productivity, and high performance.

 - Strategic Planning: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define and execute the technical roadmap, aligning with Binti’s mission and business objectives.

 - Product Development: Oversee the end-to-end development process, ensuring the delivery of high-quality, user-friendly software that addresses the unique challenges of the child welfare space.

 - Innovation: Stay ahead of emerging technologies and industry best practices to drive innovation within the engineering team, contributing to the long-term success and scalability of Binti’s platform.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with product managers, designers, and other stakeholders to translate business requirements into technical solutions, with a focus on user experience and system performance.

 - Agile Methodology: Implement and champion agile development methodologies, ensuring the team operates efficiently and adapts to evolving project needs.


SAMPLE PROJECTS

 - Facilitate the expansion of our AI Package by consistently surfacing our AI features across our platform.

 - Leading architectural improvements to the platform capabilities at Binti’s foundation, allowing for a cohesive product experience across all the modules.

 - Taking Binti’s next new module from 0 to 1 and beyond


TECH STACK

 - Ruby on Rails

 - React

 - GraphQL

 - Postgres

 - Kubernetes & GCP


WHAT WE VALUE

 - Team Leadership: Demonstrated success in leading and managing engineering teams to high performance, ideally in a SaaS or tech start-up environment with at least 5-7 years of management experience.

 - Technical Expertise: Strong technical background with experience in full-stack development, cloud computing, and scalable architecture.

 - Strategic Thinker: Ability to align technical strategies with business priorities and goals, contributing to the overall success and growth of the company.

 - Collaborative Approach: Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and stakeholders.

 - Passion for Social Impact: A genuine interest in leveraging technology to address social ch

source_jobs.title: Staff Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.




OVERVIEW

We're looking for a Staff Engineer who combines deep technical judgment with strong product instincts, and who is energized by applying modern AI tooling to accelerate both their own work and the broader team's output. At this level, you'll drive technical direction across teams and help define the engineering culture at Binti.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own cross-team technical strategy for major platform areas — setting direction on architecture, reliability, and scalability, not just within your squad but across engineering

 - Lead complex, multi-quarter infrastructure and architectural initiatives from design through delivery, coordinating dependencies across teams

 - Define and champion engineering standards and patterns that make the codebase more flexible, scalable, and maintainable for all engineers

 - Partner closely with Product, Policy, and Technical Account Management to shape roadmap decisions with strong technical and product perspective

 - Identify and resolve systemic risks — including security, HIPAA compliance, performance bottlenecks, and incident response — before they become problems

 - Raise the technical ceiling for the whole engineering org through code review, design critique, and modeling excellent judgment

 - Proactively leverage AI and modern tooling to drive higher output quality and team efficiency




SAMPLE PROJECTS

 - Architect a reusable, configurable platform layer for scheduling and application requirements that scales across dozens of distinct agency workflows nationwide

 - Design the data model and engineering plan for representing complex, non-biological human relationships across the platform

 - Lead the cross-team technical design for transferring applications between agencies with variable permission and workflow configurations

 - Spearhead development of new product areas like child intake and community crisis response, defining the engineering foundation others build on




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Demonstrated track record leading large-scale infrastructure and architectural projects across multiple teams or systems

 - Ability to set technical direction, not just execute on it — including navigating ambiguity, making principled trade-offs, and bringing others along

 - Deep experience building and maintaining configurable, enterprise-grade systems; government-facing software experience is a strong plus

 - Product orientation — you think about user impact and business outcomes, not just technical elegance

 - Excellent communication skills: you can distill complex technical decisions for product, engineering, and non-technical stakeholders

 - Experience mentoring and elevating senior engineers, and a genuine investment in team culture

 - Fluency applying AI tools in your own workflow and excitement abou

source_jobs.title: Staff/Principal Application Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.


As Binti's first Principal Security Engineer (Applications focused), reporting to our CTO, you will play a critical role in ensuring the security and integrity of our software applications. You will work alongside Binti’s full-stack engineers, contribute to security controls in our software, identify and address potential security vulnerabilities, implement best practices, and uphold secure coding standards.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Conduct Security Assessments: Provide holistic assessments of Binti’s security stance, including performing regular security reviews, code audits, penetration testing, and threat modeling to maintain the highest standard of application security.

 - Set Direction: Help Binti chart a specific and pragmatic course of action to achieve a strong security posture. This includes scoping and prioritizing work, determining what levels of investment and risk we should take on given our scale and capacity, contributing to job descriptions and hiring plans for the next team members, and building relationships across teams and with company leadership to eﬀectively communicate and advocate for these goals.

 - Respond To Incidents: Respond promptly to security incidents, collaborate with engineers on-call, and provide detailed post-event analyses. Evaluate the applicability of emergent security concerns through risk rating and assessment (such as OWASP).

 - Improve Security Architecture: In a leadership capacity with the Engineering team, identify, design, and implement technologies to enhance security automation, during the software development lifecycle, within the product itself, and in cloud hosting environments.

 - Set Security Standards: Lead eﬀorts to design and implement secure coding standards and best practices across the development lifecycle, with an eye toward automation, including effective AI tools

 - Share Expertise: Stay up to date on the latest security threats, vulnerabilities, and industry best practices, and ensure the integration of this knowledge into Binti’s security strategies. Act as our company’s expert on application security matters, providing mentorship to development teams and fostering a scalable, security-aware culture.

 - Represent the Security team to other Binti teams and Binti leadership: Act as a steward of the Binti values. Tell the story of the security team, advertising its good work, and celebrating wins.



SAMPLE PROJECTS

 - Review and implement security patches and hotfixes in production applications.

 - Implement streamlined feedback of security recommendations for new products before launch into the Binti platform.

 - Improve the security of documents and files uploaded and downloaded on the platform.

 - Analysis, scoping, and implementation of security improvements to better protect Personal Health I

source_jobs.title: Finance and Accounting Associate
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.



OVERVIEW OF ROLE

Binti is seeking an analytical and strategic Finance and Accounting Associate to join our team. This role is ideal for a detail-oriented professional with a background in finance and accounting, who is ready to bring their expertise to a fast-paced, mission-driven environment. As a Finance and Accounting Associate, you will not only oversee essential administrative finance functions—such as invoicing, insurance management, and processing payments—but also lead initiatives to streamline and innovate our finance and operational processes.

Beyond these core responsibilities, the Finance and Accounting Associate will play a pivotal role in driving data-driven projects. Your analytical insights and process improvement strategies will directly influence Binti’s financial and operational efficiency, making a lasting impact on our mission to deliver excellence. This role offers a unique opportunity to blend hands-on financial management with high-level strategic planning.



LOCATION

Binti is headquartered in San Francisco, California, and requires team members to be in-office at least Mondays and Thursdays.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Data Analytics: Contribute to our internal data infrastructure through automated data extraction and transformation. Write queries and create dashboards to deliver insights to internal teams.

 - Accounts Receivables Management: Generate customer invoices, follow up on payments, apply payments to invoices in Maxio and communicate with customers on overdue balances.

 - Account Reconciliation: Reconcile customer accounts and resolve any discrepancies or issues that arise.

 - Employee Expense Processing: Review and process employee expense submissions via Ramp, including home office stipends, work-related travel, and other reimbursements.

 - Bank and investor reporting: Assist in preparing monthly compliance reports and quarterly investor updates.

 - Process Payroll: Process hourly and salaried payroll including calculating commission payments.

 - Reporting and Analysis: Prepare regular reports on accounts receivable status, aging, and collections activities. Also, support the CFO in preparing cash flow projections and various financial health scenarios.



SAMPLE PROJECTS

 - Write SQL queries to generate reports and build dashboards from our production database.

 - Create saved materialized views to improve data accessibility and enable self-service reporting for teams.

 - Add rigor to invoicing practices through documentation of unique customer requirements.

 - Formalize contract renewal tracking to improve stakeholder visibility and financial accuracy.

 - Assist in the development of departmental income statements.

 - Improve ARR tracking process and documentation.



WHAT WE LOVE ABOUT YOU

Qualifications:

 - Educati

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.






WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Drive our vision of finding every child a permanent and loving family and improving outcomes across child welfare by representing Binti's design philosophy internally and externally.

 - Own design end-to-end across Binti's full product suite — from Licensing and Family Finding to Service Referrals, our AI Package, and beyond.

 - Collaborate with product managers in early-stage research, ideation, and problem-solving to deeply understand user pain points (caseworkers, agency leadership, families, and youth) and develop solutions that fit the realities of their work.

 - Design and iterate on wireframes and high-fidelity mockups, coaching product managers to think through both the north star and the MVP.

 - Rapidly prototype concepts using AI design tools (e.g., Lovable, Figma Make) to validate ideas with users and stakeholders before engineering invests, and to communicate vision in a way static mocks can't.

 - Own and level up our design system so it scales with our growing product surface area.

 - Stay on top of trends, accessibility standards (we serve government users, so WCAG matters), and research best practices to inform design direction.

 - Iterate on and improve processes between design, product, and engineering to improve effectiveness as we grow.






WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of experience in UX, UI, or product design. Experience working with a complex product or problem space (B2B, enterprise, or government software) is preferred.

 - Expertise with AI design and prototyping tools — you're already fluent with tools like Lovable, Claude, Cursor, or similar, and you treat them as a core part of your craft. You can go from a rough idea to a working interactive prototype in a single sitting, and you have strong opinions about when to reach for which tool.

 - The ability to dig into the root of the problem and understand user needs and pain points — especially for users whose workflows are unfamiliar or constrained (e.g., social workers operating under significant regulatory and emotional load).

 - Strong UX skills, workflow mapping, and wireframing — both on top of existing designs and from the ground up, while keeping consistency and existing design patterns in mind.

 - A track record of mentoring teammates and implementing processes that scale with a growing organization.

 - A low-ego, collaborative approach toward working with product and engineering teams.

 - Bonus: experience designing for accessibility and WCAG compliance, or for users in government, healthcare, or other regulated environments.






LOCATION

Binti is headquartered in San Francisco, California, and this role is based in our San Francisco office on a hybrid schedule — in-person Mondays and Thursdays, with flexibility to work remotely the rest of

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager, Licensing
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.




SCOPE

There is a critical shortage of foster and adoptive families in the U.S. The path to becoming licensed — paperwork, home studies, training, references, background checks — has long been slow, opaque, and built around the agency rather than the family. Many prospective caregivers drop out before approval, and overstretched social workers spend their time chasing forms instead of supporting families.



Binti's Caregiver Licensing module is our flagship product. It powers a fully digital, mobile-friendly application experience with e-signatures, document uploads, automated reminders, and configurable forms tailored to each agency. Agencies on Licensing approve 30% more families in their first year, with a 16% reduction in time to approve. Over 100,000 families have been licensed through it.






ABOUT THE ROLE

Binti PMs drive the strategy and execution of Binti's roadmap. You'll partner closely with Engineering, Sales, and customer-facing teams, alongside an experienced and ambitious group of fellow PMs. We're looking for a Senior Product Manager to own Licensing's next chapter — smarter, more configurable, and more tightly integrated with the rest of Binti. This is a chance to scale and improve a product that already powers nearly half the country's child welfare system. This role reports to our Head of Product.






WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own product strategy and roadmap for the Licensing product.

 - Translate the needs of social workers, agency leadership, families, and kinship caregivers into a clear multi-year vision and a sharp quarterly roadmap.

 - Drive the metrics that matter: families licensed, time to approval, applicant drop-off, social worker time saved, and equity of approval rates.

 - Identify where AI can transform licensing workflows — automated document review, intelligent form pre-fill, draft home study narratives, smart caseload prioritization — and partner with engineering to ship responsibly.

 - Prototype rapidly using AI tools (Lovable, Claude, Cursor, v0) to test ideas with users in days, not weeks. We expect PMs to use AI aggressively in their own workflow.

 - Spend real time shadowing social workers, supervisors, and families going through the process — deep user understanding is non-negotiable here.

 - Push configurability and scale. Licensing has to flex to every state and county we serve while staying coherent and minimizing technical and operational overhead.






WHAT WE LOVE ABOUT YOU

 - 5+ years of product management experience, preferably at a high-growth B2B or enterprise SaaS company. Experience with regulated, government-facing, or complex workflow software is a plus.

 - A track record of scaling products meaningfully — taking something from "good and growing" to category-defining.

 - AI-native in how you work and w

source_jobs.title: Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.





ROLE OVERVIEW

Binti is looking for a full-cycle Recruiter to support our growing team. We’re hiring across a number of functions and need someone who can jump in, move fast, and execute at high volume with great attention to detail.

You will partner closely with members of the executive team and our hiring managers to execute Binti’s recruiting process and bring exceptional, diverse talent to our mission-driven team. In this role, you will support the full recruitment lifecycle, from sourcing to closing, ensuring an excellent experience for all candidates. You’ll learn and adopt Binti’s recruiting approach, contribute to a high-throughput hiring motion, and help build the team working to ensure every child has a safe, loving, and stable family.

This role will report to the Chief of Staff.



IN THIS ROLE, YOU WILL:

 - Execute the full-cycle recruiting process across all functions, from intake meetings and job descriptions to candidate onsites, offer development, and closing, following Binti’s established processes and leveraging AI support to move quickly and efficiently.

 - Get to know the Binti product and our customers deeply so you can share our story with the world, creating lifelong advocates as you connect with future Bintians.

 - Source and engage a high volume of qualified candidates to build a diverse pipeline using primarily outbound outreach, partnering closely with stakeholders to refine sourcing approaches for each role.

 - Support hiring managers on Binti’s interviewing and assessment practices, equitable hiring, and compensation discussions, helping keep the process moving quickly and consistently.

 - Ensure an excellent candidate experience by communicating clearly and promptly, managing interview schedules efficiently, and championing Binti’s values and mission throughout the process.

 - Track and report on key recruiting metrics to help the team measure progress, flag bottlenecks, and ensure hiring velocity stays on track.



ABOUT YOU

 - You have at least 1-2 years of experience as a sourcer, recruiter, or similar role where you successfully learned existing processes and performed at a high level of throughput and efficiency.

 - You are a clear and influential communicator both verbally and in writing, able to keep candidates engaged and work collaboratively with hiring managers throughout the process.

 - You demonstrate excellent organization and execution skills, moving with urgency and following through on commitments and timelines. You thrive in a high-volume environment and stay organized even when juggling many open roles.

 - You excel at stakeholder management, partnering with hiring managers across functions and keeping candidates informed throughout the process. You know how to navigate competing priorities while keeping thi

source_jobs.title: Product Lead, AI & Platform
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.




SCOPE

Social work is one of the most document-intensive and high-stakes professions in the country. Social workers often spend more time on paperwork and data entry than they do with the families they serve. At Binti, we believe technology should be a multiplier for human empathy, not a barrier to it.

The AI & Platform team owns the foundational engine that powers every module at Binti—from Licensing and Placements to Family Finding and Service Referral Management. This team is responsible for Binti AI, our suite of responsible, human-led AI tools like transcription, translation, and case-specific chat that dramatically reduce administrative burden. This role will also own the Core Platform, including the shared infrastructure, data models, shared components, and horizontal functionality that allow Binti to scale and grow seamlessly.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Binti PMs drive the strategy and execution of the roadmap. As the Product Lead for AI & Platform, you will own a very technically ambitious and horizontally impactful part of our product. You will partner closely with Engineering, Design, and our module-specific squads to ensure that AI and core platform capabilities are deeply integrated into every Binti user’s daily workflow.

This is a rare opportunity to define how LLMs and agentic patterns can be used responsibly in government and social services at a national scale. This role will report to our Product Leadership team.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own the AI & Platform Roadmap: Develop the multi-year vision for how AI and core platform services (e.g., global search, custom data models, forms infrastructure) will evolve to support Binti’s rapid expansion.

 - Scale Binti AI: Drive the development of tools that transform social work, such as automated meeting transcription, intelligent form pre-fill, and "Chat with Binti AI" for instant case insights, while ensuring these tools remain human-led and bias-mitigated.

 - Drive Horizontal Impact: Identify common pain points across different Binti modules and build platform-level solutions that improve the experience for social workers, no matter the workflow.

 - Partner with Engineering: Collaborate with our technical teams to architect scalable AI systems and a flexible platform that can handle complex, agency-specific configurations without increasing operational overhead.

 - Prototype Rapidly: Use AI-native tools (Lovable, Claude Code, Cursor) to build and test high-fidelity prototypes of new platform and AI features in days, not weeks.

 - User-Centric Research: Spend time shadowing social workers and agency leadership to understand how AI can solve their deepest frustrations, ensuring our technical solutions are always grounded in human needs.

 - Drive Success Horizontally: Own the metrics for platform health and

source_jobs.title: Senior People Operations Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.



Binti is looking for a Senior People Operations Coordinator dedicated to building an engaging and inclusive workplace where our team can thrive! In this role, you’ll manage the smooth running of our office space, welcome new hires through a tightly-run onboarding program, plan and execute intentional events to build belonging across the team, and develop your HR skills as you support the breadth of People Operations in a growing start-up. And at Binti, your work will matter– you will execute key People initiatives with a high degree of quality, contributing to our team's success in ensuring every child has a safe, loving, and stable family.



This role will report to the Head of People & Places and is expected to be in office in San Francisco at least every Monday and Thursday with increased presence during high-traffic times.





IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Manage our office space to maintain an excellent employee experience. Own our lunch and snack program, keep the space organized and welcoming during business hours, manage mail and deliveries, oversee our relationships with our property manager and key vendors, be the first point of contact for employee needs throughout the day, and more!

 - Own our onboarding program. Be a friendly face and first point of contact for new hires, manage the details of the onboarding calendar, own all administrative tasks related to onboarding, and bring an ownership mindset as you improve and develop the program over time.

 - Dream up and execute intentionally inclusive events and activities. From in-office happy hours to company-wide offsites to virtual team-building activities, these touchpoints will help your colleagues build lasting connections in our San Francisco office and across satellite locations. Use your creativity, team feedback, and growing knowledge of industry trends to develop programs that speak to our company culture.

 - Support the details of our People programs, using AI to extend your impact! Whether you’re reviewing I-9s, supporting recruiting coordination, managing offboarding, or driving benefits enrollments, you’ll have a hand in making our People program run smoothly. You’ll gain exposure to the breadth of the HR function and learn a lot along the way!




ABOUT YOU:

 - You have at least 2 years of experience as a People Ops Coordinator, Office Manager, Recruiting Coordinator, or other role where you successfully managed the details of multiple programs and processes.

 - You are a strong communicator verbally and in writing and back up your words with action, keeping your stakeholders informed as you make progress on your commitments.

 - You know that details matter in driving a positive employee experience and building trust, and you naturally build out processes to make sure you’re staying organiz

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Service Referral Management
source_jobs.description:
Binti builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services. We started in child welfare, with the mission of helping every child have a safe, loving, and stable family. To date, we’ve helped approve more than 100,000 families to foster or adopt, and we support over 49% of the nation’s child welfare system. We have expanded our product offerings in child welfare, moving more to the root of the problem, helping families stay together and avoid separation, and are now expanding horizontally across other areas in social services.



Binti is a for-profit, mission-driven software company based in San Francisco, CA. Investors include Founders Fund, First Round Capital, Kapor Capital, and others. We’re a team of ~90 people and growing quickly. We care about creating a workplace where everyone feels welcome and can bring their full self to work. We have a huge, ambitious vision to rewire government to be more effective in expanding opportunities for people around the world, and we are looking for mission-driven, high-empathy, high-performance, and low-ego team members to join us on our exciting journey towards that vision.




SCOPE

The majority of child welfare cases are driven by neglect, not abuse, which means the single highest-leverage intervention is keeping families together before a child ever enters the system. The Families First Prevention Services Act (FFPSA) opened the door for states to claim federal Title IV-E reimbursement for evidence-based prevention services like mental health treatment, substance use counseling, and in-home parenting programs. The catch: most states can't operationalize it. Only a handful have successfully claimed any of the available funding, and the problem is coordination, with public agencies and private providers working in disconnected systems, tracking services through email and spreadsheets, and losing critical data before it can be used for claiming.



Binti's Service Referral Management (SRM) module changes that. It creates a closed-loop referral system between public agencies and private providers, enabling agencies to 1) create, send, and receive referrals, 2) track services from intake through completion, 3) manage FFPSA eligibility and prevention plans, and 4) export clean data for federal reimbursement. SRM is Binti's newest and fastest-growing product, with significant expansion planned across our existing customer base and new states.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Binti PMs drive the strategy and execution of Binti's roadmap. You'll partner closely with Engineering, Sales, and customer-facing teams, alongside an experienced and ambitious group of fellow PMs. We're looking for a Product Manager to own SRM's next chapter — expanding to new states, deepening the closed-loop referral experience, and building toward the most effective services platform in the country. This is a chance to own a product that sits at the intersection of federal policy, complex multi-stakeholder workflows, and real impact for families.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Own product strategy and roadmap for the SRM product.

 - Translate the needs of caseworkers, agency leadership, private providers, and families into a clear multi-year vision and a sharp quarterly roadmap.

 - Drive the metrics that matter: services referred and completed, FFPSA reimbursement claimed, provider response time, family engagement, and social worker time saved.

 - Identify where AI can transform prevention workflows and partner with engineering to ship responsibly.

 - Prototype rapidly using AI tools (Lovable, Claude, Cursor, v0) to test ideas with users in days, not weeks. We expect PMs to use AI aggressively in their own workflow.

 - Shadow caseworkers, supervisors, private providers, and families. Deep user understanding is non-negotiable here, especially in a domain as complex and consequential as prevention services.

 - Push configurability and scale. SRM has to flex to every state's FFPSA prev

notes: Binti is a for-profit company that builds software for state and county government agencies, focusing on reinventing social services.
